{{Infobox character
|nom=Lory
|apparition=Appears in book 1
|status=Unknown
}}

== Biography ==

Lory is a creature that fell into the pool along with a Duck, Dodo, Eaglet, and several other animals. Upon reaching the shore with Alice leading the way, Lory participated in the consultation about how to dry. During this gathering, Lory argued with Alice, claiming that age conferred superior understanding and refusing to disclose its age. The interaction between Lory and Alice suggests a dynamic of friendly rivalry. When challenged, Lory grew sulky, and the creature reacted sharply to the Mouse's recitation of history.

== Relationships ==

'''[[Alice]]''' — friendly rivalry. Their interaction suggests a dynamic of spirited disagreement: they engaged in a lengthy argument regarding age and wisdom, with Lory asserting its seniority and refusing to disclose its age before ultimately turning sulky.

''Evolution:''
* [[Alice]] — Friendly rivalry (ch.3)
== References ==

- Alice's Adventures in Wonderland

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Secondary Characters]]
[[Category:Personnages du Tome 1]]
